arts.ttl: Live updates: Reading aim for second win in four days as Exeter City visit
arts.sum: Reading are aiming to go seven matches unbeaten as Exeter City visit the SCL Stadium. Beating Wigan Athletic 2-1 on Saturday, the Royals sit just three points outside the play-offs ahead of tonight's full League One fixture list.

arts.ttl: Reading team news: Noel Hunt named unchanged side for Exeter City visit
arts.sum: Reading boss Noel Hunt has named an unchanged side for this evening's clash with Exeter City- although there are two new names on the bench. The Royals left it late on Saturday but came from behind to beat Wigan Athletic 2-1 at the Brick Community Stadium, a sixth match unbeaten. Joel Pereira remains between the sticks, aiming for a second successive home clean sheet after the stalemate with Birmingham City last time out, while the back four remains Tivonge Rushesha, Tyler Bindon, Amadou Mbengue and Andre Garcia. Lewis Wing keeps the armband in midfield, with club captain Andy Yiadom on the bench, alongside Charlie Savage and Harvey Knibbs.

arts.ttl: EFL confirm dates for League One final day- including possible Reading play-off games
arts.sum: The English Football League have confirmed the dates for the end of the season, including the play-offs. Reading sit ninth in League One, three points outside the play-offs, with 13 matches remaining. The Royals host Barnsley on the final day, who currently sit only one point and one place below Noel Hunt's side. On Saturday, May 3, all three of the Championship, League One and League Two will reach their conclusions. While the second tier kicks off at 12.30pm, with the third and fourth tiers remaining 3pm kick offs.

arts.ttl: From Munich to Florida: Mamadi Camara debut XI as winger celebrates Reading milestone
arts.sum: Reading winger Mamadi Camara celebrated his 50th appearance for the club in Saturday's 2-1 win at Wigan Athletic. Starting out wide in place of Chem Campbell, the 21-year-old made his debut off the bench back in January 2021 for Veljko Paunovic in an FA Cup third round defeat to Luton Town, held behind-closed-doors due to the Covid-19 pandemic. In the four years that have followed, the Guinea-Bissau international has struggled for regular game time but scored four goals last season and has another four so far this campaign. Take a look below at the side named by Paunovic at Kenilworth Road back in 2021 and where they are now.

arts.ttl: Exeter City boss calling on players to show 'personality' ahead of Reading trip
arts.sum: Exeter City boss Gary Caldwell is calling on his side to be 'brave in possession' as they look to end Reading's six game unbeaten run this evening. The Grecians, who lost the reverse fixture 2-1 in August, sit down in 19th and just three points clear of the relegation zone. On Saturday, they scored an 87th-minute equaliser to draw 1-1 with Northampton Town at St James Park while the Royals netted a late winner to beat Wigan Athletic 2-1 at the Brick Community Stadium. Royals boss Noel Hunt has called on his players to thrive with the 'underdog' tag, currently just three points outside the play-off places.

arts.ttl: Sonny Cox's long-awaited goal could kick-start Exeter City striker's season
arts.sum: Injuries have meant it has been a stop-start season for the Exeter City striker, but his first goal in almost a year which rescued a point for the Grecians in their 1-1 League One draw with relegation rivals Northampton Town at St James Park could be the kick-start he needed. “Second half last season he came alive, he has had niggling injuries, been stop-start, showed glimpses we know he has, and when he got the chance, he scored.

arts.ttl: Reading boss calls on 'surprise package' to 'enjoy pressure' of promotion attempt
arts.sum: Reading boss Noel Hunt is asking his squad to 'enjoy' the pressure of challenging for promotion heading into the final 13 League One matches. Winning 2-1 at Wigan Athletic on Saturday, a sixth match without defeat, only three points separates the Royals and a play-off spot. Already earning more points at this stage than they achieved last year, Reading last finished in the top half of any division during the 2020/21 Championship season, with Andy Yiadom the only remaining player in the squad. For a young and inexperienced squad used to fighting relegation, Hunt is wanting his squad to thrive on being the 'surprise package' of the season.

arts.ttl: Reading confirm short-term signing of former Tottenham and QPR midfielder
arts.sum: Reading boss Noel Hunt has confirmed that they are on the look out for a free agent midfield addition following the injury to Michael Craig. Despite the 21-year-old playing full-back this season, the midfielder would have played more regularly in the middle of the park following the return to fitness of club captain Andy Yiadom. Now out for the season, the likes of Lewis Wing, Harvey Knibbs and Charlie Savage need midfield assistance to keep their energy levels going for a play-off push in the final 13 matches, currently three points off the top six in ninth. Take a look below at three possible options the Royals could look at for an immediate impact.

arts.ttl: 'They all contributed': Reading boss praises impact of substitutes in Wigan victory
arts.sum: Reading boss Noel Hunt praised the impact of his substitutes in Saturday's 2-1 victory over Wigan Athletic. The Royals impressed throughout at the Brick Community Stadium but fell behind in the second half to Jason Kerr's header. Digging deep to turn the tie around and keep up their six match unbeaten run, Jayden Wareham and Tyler Bindon completed the comeback which sees the club remain in the hunt for the play-offs. Adrian Akande, Kelvin Abrefa, Andy Yiadom and Louie Holzman came off the bench and all made a key contribution with blocks, tackles and runs up in Lancashire.

arts.ttl: Controversial former Reading chief executive departs Saudi Arabia after 18 months
arts.sum: Former Reading chief executive, Ron Gourlay, has confirmed that he will depart Saudi Arabian side Al-Ahli next month. The vastly-experienced businessman has over 25 years in football, making his name in a five-year stint at Chelsea. Arriving with the Royals in 2017, his time was an unmitigated disaster, linked heavily to high wages and a disenfranchised staff. His former colleague at Reading, Brian Tevreden, said: "Ron Gourlay wanted to sit in my chair. That was the difference. He made the calls on renewing contracts for players and then after a year cancelled the contracts- financially it’s unbelievable. The other thing is he brought players in on big wages. That was not my idea, and I was not happy with that. I can go on and on."
